san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings through multiple platforms including TV, online streams, and phone options. Live translations were available in over 50 languages for those who needed them.
keyTopics: How to watch the meeting live (Cable 26, YouTube, Zoom, or phone)
Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Meeting access options for residents without internet
Note: No public comment period scheduled for this meeting

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was scheduled for March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. Residents who had business before the zoning board will need to wait until the next hearing, which is rescheduled for April 8, 2026.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The group approved previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and services.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding available for affordable housing projects
Community development block grant (CDBG) applications and eligibility
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February 25, 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching a program that lets businesses catch up on unpaid licenses without penalties, reaching out to shopping centers to participate. They also discussed plans for a summer campaign to encourage residents to shop locally, partnering with private companies to sponsor city events, and offering financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - businesses can clear back payments without penalties
Shopping center outreach - getting malls and plazas to join city initiatives
Summer Shop Local campaign - encouraging residents to support Cupertino businesses
Public-Private Partnerships for city events - companies sponsoring local celebrations
Economic incentives for new retail and startups - attracting business growth

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting where they postponed several items for later discussion. The council voted to address overgrown weeds on properties around the city and invited residents to weigh in on how federal housing funds should be spent in the coming year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program - city will deal with overgrown properties
Federal housing grants for 2026-27 - public input on spending priorities
Multiple agenda items delayed to future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements that help the city operate better. They approved a deal with the American Red Cross to use city buildings during emergencies, and authorized a three-year software contract with Insight Public Sector for email security services costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency facility agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
